assertions.title: totus auth login saves API key

steps.observed: Config backed up successfully; existing config had api_url set

steps.action: Pipe API key via echo to auth login --stdin
steps.observed: Output: '✓ Authenticated as test (key: ********************)\n  API key stored in /Users/weseklund/.config/totus/config.json'. Exit code 0.

steps.expected: Config contains api_key field with the provided key

steps.action: Test format validation with invalid key
steps.observed: Output: '✗ Error: Invalid API key format\n  Expected format: tot_live_{8 chars}_{32 chars}'. Exit code 2.
Config restored to original state (api_url only, no api_key)

terminal: echo 'tot_live_Oxcn5bC8_tRl6NOd4rNEVB19W8pXppzS9M55B6nPC' | bun run packages/cli/src/index.ts auth login --stdin --server-url http://localhost:3000/api -> exit 0, '✓ Authenticated as test'
cat ~/.config/totus/config.json -> api_key field present with correct key
echo 'invalid_key_format' | bun run packages/cli/src/index.ts auth login --stdin --server-url http://localhost:3000/api -> exit 2, '✗ Error: Invalid API key format'

assertions.title: totus profile shows user display name and health data summary

steps.action: Run totus profile with API key via env var
steps.expected: User display name and health data summary displayed

terminal: TOTUS_API_KEY=tot_live_Oxcn5bC8_tRl6NOd4rNEVB19W8pXppzS9M55B6nPC bun run packages/cli/src/index.ts profile --server-url http://localhost:3000/api -> exit 0
Output shows display_name='test', total_data_points=720, active_shares=2, connections=1
evidence.network: GET /api/user/profile -> 200 (via API key auth with getResolvedContext)

assertions.title: totus export downloads JSON data export

steps.action: Run totus export with API key via env var and --file flag
steps.observed: Error: 'API error (500): An unexpected error occurred'. Exit code 1. No file created.

steps.action: Test export API directly with curl and API key auth
Test export API with session cookie auth (to rule out auth issues)
steps.observed: 500 with same INTERNAL_ERROR — confirms the error is server-side, NOT auth-related
Confirmed 720 rows exist with valid encrypted values (95 bytes each)

evidence.consoleErrors: Server-side unhandled error (logged to Next.js console as 'Unhandled error:')
terminal: TOTUS_API_KEY=... bun run packages/cli/src/index.ts export --server-url http://localhost:3000/api --file /tmp/totus-test-export.json -v -> exit 1, '→ POST http://localhost:3000/api/user/export ← 500 Internal Server Error'
evidence.network: POST /api/user/export -> 500 (both API key and session auth)

assertions.issues: The export API route returns 500 Internal Server Error. This is NOT caused by the auth fix (getResolvedContext works correctly — confirmed by profile command success and the fact that session-based auth also fails with the same 500). The likely cause is a decryption error in the export route when iterating over 720 health_data_daily rows — possibly ENCRYPTION_KEY mismatch between seed time and current server process, or a data serialization issue. The CLI correctly sends POST (not GET) and the route uses getResolvedContext(), confirming both fixes from commit 6ab327e are in place. The 500 is a separate pre-existing server-side bug in the export data pipeline.

frictions.description: auth login does not support --api-key flag for non-interactive login; uses --stdin flag instead to read key from piped input
frictions.resolution: Used 'echo <key> | totus auth login --stdin' pattern to pipe the key non-interactively

frictions.description: In non-TTY mode (piped output), profile command outputs JSON instead of formatted table — this is by design per CLI TTY detection
frictions.resolution: JSON output still contains all required fields (display_name, stats with data_points/connections/active_shares)

blockers.description: POST /api/user/export returns 500 Internal Server Error regardless of auth method (API key or session cookie). The error is server-side (not auth-related) — likely a decryption failure when processing the 720 health_data_daily rows for export.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Tested with both API key auth and session cookie auth. Also verified database has 720 valid health_data_daily rows with 95-byte encrypted values. Both auth methods produce the same 500, confirming the issue is in the export data pipeline, not authentication.

summary: Tested 3 assertions: 2 passed (VAL-CLI-002 auth login, VAL-CLI-016 profile), 1 failed (VAL-CLI-017 export — 500 server error unrelated to auth fix). The getResolvedContext() fix from commit 6ab327e is confirmed working for auth login and profile. The export 500 is a separate server-side bug (decryption/serialization issue) affecting both API key and session auth.
